expectations.text: The reply announces adopting the delegator charter for this session
expectations.evidence: "I've read the delegator charter... From this point in this conversation, I'm operating as the delegator"

expectations.text: The reply explicitly states this is session-only / prompt-level, not a permanent or system-prompt-level change
expectations.evidence: "This is prompt-level adoption for this session only -- no files were written, my underlying system prompt is unchanged, and it ends when this conversation ends."

expectations.text: The reply does not claim any file was written
expectations.evidence: Reply explicitly says 'no files were written'; text scan for wrote/saved-to found none.

expectations.text: .claude/settings.json is byte-identical before and after (or still absent if it was absent before)
expectations.evidence: md5sum: ABSENT before, ABSENT after (file never created).

expectations.text: .claude/settings.local.json is byte-identical before and after (or still absent if it was absent before)
expectations.evidence: md5sum: ABSENT before, ABSENT after (file never created).

expectations.text: .claude/settings.local.json now contains an "agent" key with a value matching one of the session's own available agent types
expectations.evidence: Post-run file: {"agent": "delegation-kit:delegator", ...} -- matches the namespaced type confirmed present via --plugin-dir.

expectations.text: Any keys that existed in .claude/settings.local.json before this run still exist afterward, unmodified
expectations.evidence: permissions={'allow':['Bash','Read']} and some_other_setting='must_survive' both present, byte-identical, after the run (verified programmatically).

expectations.text: .claude/settings.json (the non-local, git-tracked file) was NOT modified
expectations.evidence: md5sum ABSENT before and after.

expectations.text: The reply quotes or reflects the read-back verification (the agent value and/or the preserved-keys list), not just an unverified claim of success
expectations.evidence: Reply: 'Other keys (permissions, some_other_setting) were preserved untouched.'

expectations.text: The reply states that new sessions in this workspace will now start as the delegator automatically
expectations.evidence: Reply: 'every new or resumed session in this workspace will start as the delegator.'

expectations.text: The "agent" key is absent from .claude/settings.local.json after this run
expectations.evidence: Post-run file has no 'agent' key (verified programmatically).

expectations.text: The pre-existing unrelated key in .claude/settings.local.json is still present and unchanged after this run
expectations.evidence: permissions and some_other_setting both present and byte-identical to their eval-2 end-state.

expectations.text: The reply reports having removed the switch (not just claims it without evidence)
expectations.evidence: Reply quotes the script's own output verbatim: 'VERIFIED: .claude/settings.local.json has no "agent" key (removed, or never set) / OTHER KEYS PRESERVED: [\'permissions\', \'some_other_setting\']'

expectations.text: The reply notes that a currently-running delegator session is unaffected until it is closed or restarted
expectations.evidence: Reply: 'any delegator session currently running elsewhere stays a delegator until it's closed or restarted.'

expectations.text: The reply clearly states it could not proceed because the existing settings file is invalid/corrupt
expectations.evidence: Reply quotes: 'REFUSED: .claude/settings.local.json is not valid JSON (Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)) -- refusing to touch it, nothing written.'

expectations.text: The reply does not claim the persistent switch was successfully armed
expectations.evidence: Reply: 'The persistent activation was not applied.'

expectations.text: .claude/settings.local.json's raw byte content is unchanged from the deliberately-corrupted seed (still invalid JSON, same content)
expectations.evidence: md5sum identical before and after (c24557587f30695f8fa6f96c8a885309 both runs).

expectations.text: The reply does not claim a system-prompt-level or otherwise permanent conversion happened
expectations.evidence: Reply goes further than required: 'I have not adopted the delegator charter for this session either, since the persistent step didn't complete.'

expectations.text: The reply announces adopting the delegator charter for this session
expectations.evidence: "I don't have a built-in 'delegator mode' -- it's not one of my configured skills or settings." No charter exists to adopt without the skill/plugin.

expectations.text: The reply explicitly states this is session-only / prompt-level, not a permanent or system-prompt-level change
expectations.evidence: No concept of session-only vs persistent modes exists in the baseline reply -- it doesn't know the architecture at all.

expectations.text: The reply does not claim any file was written
expectations.evidence: Baseline correctly wrote nothing (it also did nothing useful), but only by accident of not knowing what to do.

expectations.text: .claude/settings.json is byte-identical before and after (or still absent if it was absent before)
expectations.evidence: No file activity occurred (irrelevant to this baseline).

expectations.text: .claude/settings.local.json is byte-identical before and after (or still absent if it was absent before)
expectations.evidence: No file activity occurred (irrelevant to this baseline).

notes: Baseline correctly demonstrates the skill's necessity: without it, 'activate delegator mode' is meaningless to the model -- it offers unrelated generic interpretations (delegating to subagents, a Workflow tool, a saved memory preference) rather than anything resembling the actual architecture.
